summary: US-845 component regression verification passed for the default component desktop table UI scope. Mandatory Spexus current_context and task reads were completed. Exact dependency reports for MZ-501, MZ-502, MZ-503, MZ-504, accepted MZ-505 attempt 3, and MZ-505 fix-2 closure were present with required report fields, successful/accepted statuses, risks=[], and failure_type=not_applicable. The collective evidence covers desktop table states only: bidding, contract/order, whist, play, deal-result/settlement, final-result, plus jsdom coverage for next-deal and supporting contract sub-states. Tutorial, glossary, and full-mobile scope were not added; matching terms appear only as explicit out-of-scope notes. MZ-505 fix-2 visual evidence closes the prior contract action visibility and settlement/final readability issues, and MZ-504 accessibility evidence remains applicable because fix-2 preserved native buttons, accessible names, labeled state/result sections, and focus-visible styling. The release gate is satisfied by same-day fix-2 npm run verify evidence plus regenerated MZ-505 Playwright screenshots/results. Screenshot evidence is synthetic/static UI evidence rendered from deterministic fixtures, not backend-live evidence.

tests_run.evidence: Read active Spexus tenant/project/component scope before reading task artifact; active component was default in project PERSONAL.
Confirmed task [MZ-506] US-845 component regression verification under epic PERSONAL-EP-180 with desktop table states only, release gate required, and dependencies MZ-502 through MZ-505.

tests_run.command: ls task-reports/PERSONAL-TASK-1161 task-reports/PERSONAL-TASK-1162 task-reports/PERSONAL-TASK-1163 task-reports/PERSONAL-TASK-1164 task-reports/PERSONAL-TASK-1165 task-reports
tests_run.evidence: Confirmed all exact dependency report paths named by the verifier prompt are present, including MZ-505 attempt-1/attempt-2 context, accepted attempt-3, and fix-2 closure evidence.

Reports contained task_id, epic_id, status, summary, files_changed, tests_run, risks, failure_type, verification_environment, evidence_matrix, and quality_gate_matrix where applicable. MZ-501/MZ-502/MZ-503 status=succeeded, MZ-504/MZ-505 attempt-3 status=passed, MZ-505 fix-2 status=fixed; all had risks=[] and failure_type=not_applicable.

tests_run.command: Source and test inspection: docs/test-design/us-845-desktop-table-states.md; src/ui/render.ts; src/styles.css; src/ui/render.test.ts; src/ui/controller.test.ts; src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts; output/playwright/mz505-capture.mjs
tests_run.evidence: Confirmed owner scope is desktop/laptop table UI states; renderer/tests cover state context, legal actions, score/result panels, contract-order action grid, accessible labels, focus styles, deal-result/next-deal/final flows, and deterministic capture fixtures.

tests_run.command: rg -n "tutorial|glossary|full-mobile|mobile|help|справ|словар|обуч" docs/test-design/us-845-desktop-table-states.md src/ui/render.ts src/styles.css src/ui/render.test.ts src/ui/controller.test.ts src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts output/playwright/mz505-capture.mjs task-reports/PERSONAL-TASK-116[1-5] task-reports/orchestrator-log.json
tests_run.evidence: No production/test implementation added tutorial, glossary, or full-mobile scope. Hits were explicit out-of-scope statements in the design/report artifacts or existing responsive CSS references.

All six captured state families at desktop and laptop reported horizontalOverflow=false and score panel present. Contract actionAreaRect.bottom was 581.08 desktop and 573.00 laptop, below statusLineTop 749.81/719.08. Settlement/final resultPanelRect.bottom was 608.48/628.72 desktop and 679.25/643.84 laptop, below statusLineTop 749.81/719.08. The only clippedText entry across relevant captures was the known decorative MARRIAGE heading.

tests_run.command: Visual inspection of output/playwright/mz-505-contract-laptop.png, mz-505-settlement-laptop.png, mz-505-final-laptop.png, mz-505-bidding-desktop.png, and mz-505-play-desktop.png
tests_run.evidence: Verified contract-order actions are visible in a compact grid; settlement and final result panels are readable and not crowded by inactive hand rows; bidding and play desktop states show the table, score panel, state rail, active phase/actor, and legal action/card affordances.

fix-2 records npm run verify passed on 2026-07-06 with Vitest 14 files / 136 tests, TypeScript compilation, and Vite production build passing, followed by regenerated MZ-505 screenshots and capture results. This verifier did not rerun npm run verify because dependency/source/visual evidence was sufficient.

verification_environment.evidence_classification: Screenshots are synthetic/static visual UI evidence rendered through the local Vite app with deterministic GameState fixtures; they are not backend-live evidence.

commands_not_rerun.reason: Same-day MZ-505 fix-2 closure records npm run verify passed after the final UI/layout fix and regenerated screenshot evidence. Local report/source/metric evidence was sufficient, so the verifier did not spend additional runtime.
MZ-505 fix-2 closure already regenerated the exact required screenshots and capture JSON; this verifier inspected those artifacts instead of rerunning the browser capture.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Desktop table UI shows current phase, active actor, legal human actions, player hands, score table, contract/trump/current trick where applicable, deal result, and final result.
evidence_matrix.findings: Renderer, jsdom tests, integration tests, accessibility review, and regenerated visual captures collectively cover the desktop table presentation contract. All captured states keep score and state context visible.
evidence_matrix.limitations: Screenshot evidence is deterministic static UI evidence, not backend-live evidence.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Bidding, contract, whist, play, deal-result, and final-result desktop table states remain understandable and do not block normal play.
evidence_matrix.findings: The exact screenshot families bidding, contract, whist, play, settlement/deal-result, and final are present for desktop and laptop. Metrics show no horizontal overflow and action/result panels above the status band. Play state uses enabled hand cards as legal action affordances, matching the existing UI contract.
evidence_matrix.limitations: The visual capture harness uses synthetic fixture states.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Deal-result and next-deal states make the outcome understandable and expose proceed action only when the bullet is not complete; final-result exposes no continuation controls.
evidence_matrix.findings: Integration tests cover settlement, next-deal, and finished result behavior. MZ-505 visual evidence confirms settlement and final result panels remain readable at desktop/laptop and are not crowded by inactive hands.
evidence_matrix.limitations: Next-deal is covered by jsdom/integration evidence, not by a dedicated MZ-505 screenshot family.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Implementation stays in the presentation-client desktop table scope and adds state clarity without changing rule/scoring behavior.
evidence_matrix.findings: MZ-502 changed renderer/styles/render tests only and reported npm run verify passed. Source shows state context, compact score/result panels, and contract action grouping in UI rendering code.
evidence_matrix.limitations: MZ-502 itself did not add fresh screenshot evidence; downstream MZ-505 supplied it.

evidence_matrix.scenario: US-scoped integration evidence covers representative desktop state families and legal action projection.
evidence_matrix.findings: Integration tests cover bidding, contract/order, whist, play, all-pass, misere, deal settlement, next-deal, and final result using real GameState transitions and renderer output.
evidence_matrix.limitations: Integration evidence is Vitest/jsdom, not browser viewport automation.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Accessibility evidence remains applicable after MZ-505 fix-2 visual/layout changes.
evidence_matrix.findings: MZ-504 verified native buttons, accessible action/card labels, aria-pressed discard toggles, labeled state/score/result sections, focus-visible styles, and no misleading final controls. fix-2 preserves those contracts while changing contract action layout.
evidence_matrix.limitations: No manual screen-reader run was performed by MZ-504 or this verifier.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Accepted visual regression evidence closes action visibility and result/final readability issues.
evidence_matrix.findings: Orchestrator routed risk from MZ-505 attempt 2 is marked closed. fix-2 reports contract actions moved from overflowing to actionAreaRect.bottom 581.08 desktop / 573.00 laptop, with status line top 749.81 / 719.08. Settlement/final laptop result panels remain above status line with no human hand row.
evidence_matrix.limitations: Closure evidence is synthetic/static UI plus recorded automated tests.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Tutorial, glossary, and full-mobile scope were not added.
evidence_matrix.command_or_source: rg tutorial/glossary/full-mobile/mobile/help terms across docs, UI source/tests, MZ-501..MZ-505 reports, and orchestrator log
evidence_matrix.findings: Terms appear only in explicit out-of-scope notes or existing responsive CSS/report notes. No tutorial screens, glossary/help system, or full-mobile UX implementation was introduced.
evidence_matrix.limitations: Responsive CSS exists in the app, but US-845 evidence and implementation are scoped to desktop/laptop table states.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: Exact dependency report paths listed in verifier prompt
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: MZ-505 attempt-1 and attempt-2 were used for context only, per prompt.

quality_gate_matrix.gate: owner scope: desktop table states only
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: Laptop screenshots are included as desktop-table responsive evidence, not full-mobile UX.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: src/test/us845DesktopTable.integration.test.ts; output/playwright/mz-505-bidding-*.png; mz-505-contract-*.png; mz-505-whist-*.png; mz-505-play-*.png; mz-505-settlement-*.png; mz-505-final-*.png
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: Screenshot states use deterministic fixtures; next-deal is covered by jsdom/integration tests rather than the six-family MZ-505 screenshot set.
Contract-order visible labels are intentionally compact; accessible names remain full.
Synthetic fixture text and scores are used for screenshot evidence.
Evidence is deterministic DOM/source/test review, not manual screen-reader transcript.

quality_gate_matrix.command_or_source: task-reports/PERSONAL-TASK-1165/fix-2.json records npm run verify passed and regenerated MZ-505 screenshots/results
quality_gate_matrix.limitations: This verifier accepted current fix-2 recorded evidence and did not rerun npm run verify.
Screenshots are synthetic/static visual UI evidence, not backend-live evidence.
No auth, backend, network, persistence schema, or sensitive-data path is in this regression-verification scope.
